title: Julie's Pasta Sauce Bases
ingredients: 2 cups whole milk or half and half
1/2 cup grated fresh parmesan, asiago or Romano cheese
instructions: For Basic Marinara Sauce: Combine crushed tomatoes, olive oil, onion, and garlic in a large saucepan.
Add salt to taste and dash of cayenne pepper.
Serve over hot, cooked pasta and top with freshly grated Parmesan, Romano or Asiago cheese.
For Basic Alfredo Sauce: Heat butter in saucepan.
If using onion, add onion and sauté for 5 minutes until soft.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Add flour and stir well, cooking over medium heat while stirring for 1-2 minutes to make a roux.
Slowly add broth while stirring well.
Whisk in warmed milk (heated in microwave on high for 2 minutes).
Cook over medium heat, stirring or whisking frequently until thickened.
Add cheese and stir until well combined and melted.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
notes: Pasta is a fast meal that can incorporate almost many ingredients.
Basic Marinara Sauce additions if desired: meatballs, cooked Italian sausage, steamed zucchini or summer squash, chopped fresh spinach, sautéed mushrooms, or whatever else you desire.
Basic Alfredo Sauce can be used alone or stir in additions of choice.

Alfredo additions: 1 lb fresh spinach leaves chopped, sautéed mushrooms, lightly steamed veggies of choice (broccoli, squash, asparagus), cooked and diced chicken, cooked diced salmon, shrimp, crab or lobster, chopped fresh herbs, drained artichoke hearts, Cavendars Greek seasoning.
When a full house, Julie made pasta at least once a week.
